mapping.a_element: the multiverse: the space of all admissible analytic-choice combinations
mapping.b_element: the shot space: the space of all admissible cue strokes / ball configurations

mapping.a_element: evaluating the multiverse by its single worst-case universe (a min over the space)
mapping.b_element: measuring the Lebesgue measure / Monte-Carlo probability of clearance strokes over the shot space

brief_justification: The only real overlap is the loose notion of a 'space of possibilities' (analytic multiverse vs. billiard shot space); the governing machinery differs entirely. Paper A is a normative meta-science argument proposing rhetorical/evaluative conventions (judge by the worst universe, treat size as weakness), whereas paper B is a rigorous measure-theoretic and Monte-Carlo analysis of billiard dynamics (positive Lebesgue measure, P(k) decay, measure-zero vs. unobservably small), so no role-for-role governing process corresponds.
